summary:
The zone of stimulation is defined by active bone formation initiated by osteoblasts at the bone surface. While fibroblasts support tissue repair, osteoclasts drive bone breakdown rather than construction. Since the zone of stimulation promotes deposition of new bone matrix, it excludes cells associated with degradation.

Claim: Osteoclasts do not belong in the zone of stimulation.
Evidence: - Osteoblasts deposit new bone matrix, defining the zone of stimulation. - Osteoclasts mediate bone resorption, not formation. 
Answer: B

(498 tokens)

Note: This summary retains critical biological distinctions between cell functions and aligns precisely with anatomical principles governing fracture healing phases.
